subject: Databases
title: Choosing a Distributed Database
description: One-minute skill: Choosing a Distributed Database.

hook.text: Do you actually need a distributed database? If your data fits on one machine, Postgres is simpler and cheaper.

buildup.text: Distributed databases add complexity: replication lag, partitioning, consensus, failure handling. All are costs.

discovery.text: Need scale? Pick DynamoDB or Cassandra. Need SQL? CockroachDB or Spanner. Need simplicity? Postgres with replicas.

twist.text: Many 'big data' problems are actually small data with bad queries. Optimize before you distribute.

climax.text: Managed services (RDS, Aurora, Cloud SQL) give you replicas and failover without operating a distributed database.

punchline.text: Don't distribute prematurely. Most apps scale fine on one well-tuned database.

quiz.question: When should you consider a distributed database?
options: When your data truly exceeds what a single well-tuned server can handle, after optimizing queries first
Whenever you have more than 100 users
